one_time_description: Settlement Proceeds - First Nations Drinking Water ($103,145 in 2024; $500,000 in 2023)
economic_notes: First Nation is a member of Keseechiwan Holdings Limited Partnership (investment $60,000). Water treatment plant approved and under construction with total budget of $8,789,692, $1,088,811 incurred to date. New Aboriginal Head Start on Reserve facility approved with $2,615,102 received and contract signed for $2,693,000. Bison herd valued at $170,050. File Hills Qu'Appelle Tribal Council affiliation. Treaty 4 signatory.
notes: Province is Saskatchewan (Treaty 4), not AB. Consolidated entities include Okanese First Nation CMHC and Day Walker Home Fire Family Centre Inc. (2 subsidiaries). Prior year restated for errors: restricted cash reclassification, CMHC reserves moved from liability to equity, CMHC Phase 11 loan advances reclassified from revenue to long-term debt. Investment in Keseechiwan Holdings Limited Partnership at $60,000 (equity method not applied as First Nation does not share in profit/loss). Water treatment plant commitment of $8,789,692 in progress. Deferred revenue is very high at $10.58M representing unspent ISC funding. Long-term debt includes $329,158 CMHC Phase 11 construction advance. Total government transfers calculated as ISC $10,737,890 + CMHC $516,044 + Government of Saskatchewan $311,250 = $11,565,184 (Future Generations Foundation $257,150 included if government = $11,822,334, excluded as foundation). OSR includes lease income, interest income, settlement proceeds, investment income, rental income, other, FHQTC, First Nations Trust. No remuneration schedule included in extracted text.
